title: A cleaner route to purifying rare earth elements
excerpt: Rare earth elements like lanthanum, neodymium, and dysprosium are used to build the electric motor in your car, the LED lights in your house, and the MRI machine at your doctor’s office.
content_html:
Rare earth elements like lanthanum, neodymium, and dysprosium are used to build the electric motor in your car, the LED lights in your house, and the MRI machine at your doctor’s office. But first, they have to be mined and separated from each other. Historically, that purification has been a difficult, costly, process, relying on huge amounts of toxic chemicals.

Now, researchers in the lab of Assoc. Prof. Chong Liu at the University of Chicago Pritzker School of Molecular Engineering (UChicago PME), working with colleagues at Northwestern University and Argonne National Lab, have discovered a cleaner method to separate rare earth elements from each other.

The new approach relies on a layered form of manganese oxide—a mineral material with the right size layers to allow ions to slip in and out and to differentiate rare earth elements.

“This is the first time that people have used electrochemical intercalation and harnessed the structural characteristics to separate similar lanthanides, which are intrinsically very hard to separate,” said Liu, senior author of the new study, which published in Nature Chemical Engineering. “What’s also valuable is that we provided a lot of new understanding of how rare earth ions are interacting with this material and how we can manipulate it to better selectivity.”

“This kind of separation is competitive with other rare earth separation methods, but it’s done in water, without organic solvents,” said George Schatz, professor of chemistry at Northwestern University and a co-author of the study. “That’s a difference that could actually matter at manufacturing scale.”

This is the first time that people have used electrochemical intercalation and harnessed the structural characteristics to separate similar lanthanides, which are intrinsically very hard to separate.

Assoc. Prof. Chong Liu, senior author of the study

Squeezing elements through channels

The 17 rare earth elements—including the 15 lanthanides, plus scandium and yttrium—rarely occur alone. They’re almost always mined together and chemically they’re nearly identical, with only tiny differences in ion size and acidity differentiating each one. Pulling them apart typically requires custom-built molecules and large amounts of acid, which is used to strip each element off those molecules. 

“Rare earths always come mixed together, whether they’re in an ore or in a waste stream, and separating them from each other is a second, very challenging step even after you’ve pulled them away from everything else,” said UChicago PME graduate student Jiadong Liu, a co-first author of the new paper. 

Chong Liu and her colleagues knew that one of the differences between rare earth ions was the size of the water shell surrounding each one when they are dissolved in solution. Lighter rare earths like lanthanum have larger first water shell, while heavier rare earths like dysprosium have a smaller first shell. 

Taking advantage of that size difference, Chong Liu’s group engineered manganese oxide so that the gaps between its stacked layers were only a few water molecules wide. Then, they squeezed raw mixtures of rare earth elements inside. 

The approach divided the elements into two groups. Heavier lanthanides with smaller water shells stuck in the channels more tightly. Lighter lanthanides with larger shells pushed the layers apart, loosening their grip. 

To confirm what was happening at a molecular level, the UChicago PME team collaborated with Schatz’s group at Northwestern to run quantum mechanical simulations using a method called density functional theory, which predicts how atoms arrange themselves and interact based on the underlying physics. They also worked with Argonne scientists to obtain experimental X-ray data. 

“It was incredibly rewarding to see how closely our density functional theory calculations matched the synchrotron X-ray measurements,” said co-first author Woo Cheol Jeon, who conducted the research as a postdoctoral researcher in George Schatz’s lab at Northwestern. “The calculations let us see, atom by atom, how each rare earth element arranges its hydration shell inside the confined channel, which experiments couldn’t resolve directly.”

Fine-tuning the purification

While the new process separated the heaviest and lightest rare earths, some of the most useful elements still behaved too similarly to separate. To fine-tune the purification so that it could differentiate similar pairs of rare earth elements, the research team used an electric current and added magnesium ions. 

The magnesium acted as a scaffold, holding the manganese oxide channels to their designed spacing, even when rare earth elements tried to expand it—an effect called pinning. Now, rare earth ions showed differences in binding to the layered material even when they were extremely similar. 

“Even elements that behave almost identically will still try to expand the material to make room for their water molecules,” said Siqi Zou, PhD'24, co-first author of the study and former UChicago PME graduate student. “By pinning the channel so it can’t expand at all, we forced that small difference in behavior to become a much bigger difference in how strongly each element binds.”

With the addition of magnesium, the enrichment of neodymium over lanthanum jumped from a 1.6-fold difference to a 5.4-fold difference. After two cycles of purification, researchers could get a neodymium sample that was 97% pure. Similar improvements were seen for other rare earth elements. 

This kind of separation is competitive with other rare earth separation methods, but it’s done in water, without organic solvents. That’s a difference that could actually matter at manufacturing scale.

George Schatz, professor of chemistry at Northwestern University and a co-author of the study

A cleaner future

The new purification method could ultimately point toward different ways of thinking about where rare earth processing happens, the researchers said. 

“Right now, rare earth ores get mined all over the world, but almost all of them end up being sent overseas for processing,” said Schatz, co-author of the study. “A method like this, that just uses water and electricity instead of organic solvents, is the kind of technology that could actually change how and where that processing gets done.”

The method, however, isn’t yet ready to be scaled up to replace industrial purification. Still, it points to a broader design principle: tuning a channel’s width can determine which ions a material prefers, even for ions that differ by a fraction of an angstrom. 

Chong Liu’s group is now testing the approach against more of the 15 lanthanides, while Schatz is refining his computational models, aiming to explain the pinning effect more quantitatively. He is also using the same modeling approach developed for this work to study other materials. 

Citation: “Pinning Angstrom-size solid ionic channel for the separation of rare earth elements,” Zou et al, Nature Chemical Engineering, July 21, 2026, DOI: 10.1038/s44286-026-00418-8

blocks.html_en: Rare earth elements like lanthanum, neodymium, and dysprosium are used to build the electric motor in your car, the LED lights in your house, and the MRI machine at your doctor’s office. But first, they have to be mined and separated from each other. Historically, that purification has been a difficult, costly, process, relying on huge amounts of toxic chemicals.

sentences.en: Rare earth elements like lanthanum, neodymium, and dysprosium are used to build the electric motor in your car, the LED lights in your house, and the MRI machine at your doctor’s office.
sentences.zh: 稀土元素（如镧、钕和镝）用于制造汽车中的电动机、房屋中的 LED 灯以及医生办公室中的 MRI 机器。

sentences.en: But first, they have to be mined and separated from each other.
sentences.zh: 但首先，必须对它们进行开采并相互分离。

sentences.en: Historically, that purification has been a difficult, costly, process, relying on huge amounts of toxic chemicals.
sentences.zh: 从历史上看，这种净化是一个困难且成本高昂的过程，依赖大量有毒化学品。

blocks.html_en: Now, researchers in the lab of Assoc. Prof. Chong Liu at the University of Chicago Pritzker School of Molecular Engineering (UChicago PME), working with colleagues at Northwestern University and Argonne National Lab, have discovered a cleaner method to separate rare earth elements from each other.

sentences.en: Now, researchers in the lab of Assoc.
sentences.zh: 现在，副实验室的研究人员。

sentences.en: Prof. Chong Liu at the University of Chicago Pritzker School of Molecular Engineering (UChicago PME), working with colleagues at Northwestern University and Argonne National Lab, have discovered a cleaner method to separate rare earth elements from each other.
sentences.zh: 芝加哥大学普利兹克分子工程学院 (UChicago PME) 的 Chong Liu 教授与西北大学和阿贡国家实验室的同事合作，发现了一种更清洁的方法来分离稀土元素。

blocks.html_en: The new approach relies on a layered form of manganese oxide—a mineral material with the right size layers to allow ions to slip in and out and to differentiate rare earth elements.

sentences.en: The new approach relies on a layered form of manganese oxide—a mineral material with the right size layers to allow ions to slip in and out and to differentiate rare earth elements.
sentences.zh: 新方法依赖于层状形式的氧化锰——一种具有适当尺寸层的矿物材料，允许离子滑入和滑出并区分稀土元素。

blocks.html_en: “This is the first time that people have used electrochemical intercalation and harnessed the structural characteristics to separate similar lanthanides, which are intrinsically very hard to separate,” said Liu, senior author of the new study, which published in Nature Chemical Engineering. “What’s also valuable is that we provided a lot of new understanding of how rare earth ions are interacting with this material and how we can manipulate it to better selectivity.”

sentences.en: “This is the first time that people have used electrochemical intercalation and harnessed the structural characteristics to separate similar lanthanides, which are intrinsically very hard to separate,” said Liu, senior author of the new study, which published in Nature Chemical Engineering.
sentences.zh: 这项发表在《自然化学工程》上的新研究的资深作者刘说：“这是人们第一次使用电化学插层并利用结构特征来分离类似的镧系元素，这些元素本质上很难分离。”

sentences.en: “What’s also valuable is that we provided a lot of new understanding of how rare earth ions are interacting with this material and how we can manipulate it to better selectivity.”
sentences.zh: “同样有价值的是，我们对稀土离子如何与这种材料相互作用以及我们如何操纵它以获得更好的选择性提供了许多新的理解。”

blocks.html_en: “This kind of separation is competitive with other rare earth separation methods, but it’s done in water, without organic solvents,” said George Schatz, professor of chemistry at Northwestern University and a co-author of the study. “That’s a difference that could actually matter at manufacturing scale.”

sentences.en: “This kind of separation is competitive with other rare earth separation methods, but it’s done in water, without organic solvents,” said George Schatz, professor of chemistry at Northwestern University and a co-author of the study.
sentences.zh: “这种分离方法与其他稀土分离方法具有竞争力，但它是在水中完成的，没有有机溶剂，”西北大学化学教授、该研究的合著者乔治·沙茨(George Schatz)说。

sentences.en: “That’s a difference that could actually matter at manufacturing scale.”
sentences.zh: “这种差异在制造规模上实际上可能很重要。”

sentences.en: Prof. Chong Liu, senior author of the study
sentences.zh: 该研究的资深作者Chong Liu教授

blocks.html_en: Squeezing elements through channels

sentences.en: Squeezing elements through channels
sentences.zh: 通过通道挤压元件

blocks.html_en: The 17 rare earth elements—including the 15 lanthanides, plus scandium and yttrium—rarely occur alone. They’re almost always mined together and chemically they’re nearly identical, with only tiny differences in ion size and acidity differentiating each one. Pulling them apart typically requires custom-built molecules and large amounts of acid, which is used to strip each element off those molecules.

sentences.en: The 17 rare earth elements—including the 15 lanthanides, plus scandium and yttrium—rarely occur alone.
sentences.zh: 17 种稀土元素（包括 15 种镧系元素，加上钪和钇）很少单独出现。

sentences.en: They’re almost always mined together and chemically they’re nearly identical, with only tiny differences in ion size and acidity differentiating each one.
sentences.zh: 它们几乎总是一起开采，从化学角度来看，它们几乎是相同的，只有离子大小和酸度的微小差异来区分每一种。

sentences.en: Pulling them apart typically requires custom-built molecules and large amounts of acid, which is used to strip each element off those molecules.
sentences.zh: 将它们分开通常需要定制的分子和大量的酸，酸用于从这些分子上剥离每种元素。

blocks.html_en: “Rare earths always come mixed together, whether they’re in an ore or in a waste stream, and separating them from each other is a second, very challenging step even after you’ve pulled them away from everything else,” said UChicago PME graduate student Jiadong Liu, a co-first author of the new paper.

sentences.en: “Rare earths always come mixed together, whether they’re in an ore or in a waste stream, and separating them from each other is a second, very challenging step even after you’ve pulled them away from everything else,” said UChicago PME graduate student Jiadong Liu, a co-first author of the new paper.
sentences.zh: 这篇新论文的共同第一作者、芝加哥大学 PME 研究生刘嘉栋表示：“稀土总是混合在一起，无论是在矿石中还是在废物流中，即使将它们从其他东西中分离出来，将它们彼此分离也是第二个非常具有挑战性的步骤。”

blocks.html_en: Chong Liu and her colleagues knew that one of the differences between rare earth ions was the size of the water shell surrounding each one when they are dissolved in solution. Lighter rare earths like lanthanum have larger first water shell, while heavier rare earths like dysprosium have a smaller first shell.

sentences.en: Chong Liu and her colleagues knew that one of the differences between rare earth ions was the size of the water shell surrounding each one when they are dissolved in solution.
sentences.zh: 刘冲和她的同事知道，稀土离子之间的区别之一是当它们溶解在溶液中时，每个稀土离子周围的水壳的大小。

sentences.en: Lighter rare earths like lanthanum have larger first water shell, while heavier rare earths like dysprosium have a smaller first shell.
sentences.zh: 镧等较轻的稀土元素具有较大的第一水壳层，而镝等较重的稀土元素具有较小的第一水壳层。

blocks.html_en: Taking advantage of that size difference, Chong Liu’s group engineered manganese oxide so that the gaps between its stacked layers were only a few water molecules wide. Then, they squeezed raw mixtures of rare earth elements inside.

sentences.en: Taking advantage of that size difference, Chong Liu’s group engineered manganese oxide so that the gaps between its stacked layers were only a few water molecules wide.
sentences.zh: 利用这种尺寸差异，Chong Liu 的团队设计了氧化锰，使其堆叠层之间的间隙只有几个水分子宽。

sentences.en: Then, they squeezed raw mixtures of rare earth elements inside.
sentences.zh: 然后，他们将稀土元素的原始混合物挤压到里面。

blocks.html_en: The approach divided the elements into two groups. Heavier lanthanides with smaller water shells stuck in the channels more tightly. Lighter lanthanides with larger shells pushed the layers apart, loosening their grip.

sentences.en: The approach divided the elements into two groups.
sentences.zh: 该方法将元素分为两组。

sentences.en: Heavier lanthanides with smaller water shells stuck in the channels more tightly.
sentences.zh: 较重的镧系元素和较小的水壳更紧密地卡在通道中。

sentences.en: Lighter lanthanides with larger shells pushed the layers apart, loosening their grip.
sentences.zh: 具有较大壳层的较轻的镧系元素将这些层推开，从而松开了它们的抓地力。

blocks.html_en: To confirm what was happening at a molecular level, the UChicago PME team collaborated with Schatz’s group at Northwestern to run quantum mechanical simulations using a method called density functional theory, which predicts how atoms arrange themselves and interact based on the underlying physics. They also worked with Argonne scientists to obtain experimental X-ray data.

sentences.en: To confirm what was happening at a molecular level, the UChicago PME team collaborated with Schatz’s group at Northwestern to run quantum mechanical simulations using a method called density functional theory, which predicts how atoms arrange themselves and interact based on the underlying physics.
sentences.zh: 为了确认分子水平上发生的情况，芝加哥大学 PME 团队与西北大学的 Schatz 团队合作，使用一种称为密度泛函理论的方法进行量子力学模拟，该方法根据基础物理原理预测原子如何排列自身并相互作用。

sentences.en: They also worked with Argonne scientists to obtain experimental X-ray data.
sentences.zh: 他们还与阿贡科学家合作获得实验 X 射线数据。

blocks.html_en: “It was incredibly rewarding to see how closely our density functional theory calculations matched the synchrotron X-ray measurements,” said co-first author Woo Cheol Jeon, who conducted the research as a postdoctoral researcher in George Schatz’s lab at Northwestern. “The calculations let us see, atom by atom, how each rare earth element arranges its hydration shell inside the confined channel, which experiments couldn’t resolve directly.”

sentences.en: “It was incredibly rewarding to see how closely our density functional theory calculations matched the synchrotron X-ray measurements,” said co-first author Woo Cheol Jeon, who conducted the research as a postdoctoral researcher in George Schatz’s lab at Northwestern.
sentences.zh: “看到我们的密度泛函理论计算与同步加速器 X 射线测量结果如此接近，这是令人难以置信的回报，”共同第一作者、西北大学 George Schatz 实验室的博士后研究员 Woo Cheol Jeon 说。

sentences.en: “The calculations let us see, atom by atom, how each rare earth element arranges its hydration shell inside the confined channel, which experiments couldn’t resolve directly.”
sentences.zh: “计算让我们逐个原子地了解每种稀土元素如何在有限通道内排列其水合壳，这是实验无法直接解决的。”

blocks.html_en: Fine-tuning the purification

sentences.en: Fine-tuning the purification
sentences.zh: 微调净化

blocks.html_en: While the new process separated the heaviest and lightest rare earths, some of the most useful elements still behaved too similarly to separate. To fine-tune the purification so that it could differentiate similar pairs of rare earth elements, the research team used an electric current and added magnesium ions.

sentences.en: While the new process separated the heaviest and lightest rare earths, some of the most useful elements still behaved too similarly to separate.
sentences.zh: 虽然新工艺分离了最重和最轻的稀土，但一些最有用的元素仍然表现得太相似而无法分离。

sentences.en: To fine-tune the purification so that it could differentiate similar pairs of rare earth elements, the research team used an electric current and added magnesium ions.
sentences.zh: 为了微调纯化过程，使其能够区分相似的稀土元素对，研究小组使用了电流并添加了镁离子。

blocks.html_en: The magnesium acted as a scaffold, holding the manganese oxide channels to their designed spacing, even when rare earth elements tried to expand it—an effect called pinning. Now, rare earth ions showed differences in binding to the layered material even when they were extremely similar.

sentences.en: The magnesium acted as a scaffold, holding the manganese oxide channels to their designed spacing, even when rare earth elements tried to expand it—an effect called pinning.
sentences.zh: 镁充当支架，将氧化锰通道保持在设计的间距内，即使稀土元素试图扩大它，这种效应称为钉扎。

sentences.en: Now, rare earth ions showed differences in binding to the layered material even when they were extremely similar.
sentences.zh: 现在，稀土离子在与层状材料的结合方面表现出差异，即使它们极其相似。

blocks.html_en: “Even elements that behave almost identically will still try to expand the material to make room for their water molecules,” said Siqi Zou, PhD'24, co-first author of the study and former UChicago PME graduate student. “By pinning the channel so it can’t expand at all, we forced that small difference in behavior to become a much bigger difference in how strongly each element binds.”

sentences.en: “Even elements that behave almost identically will still try to expand the material to make room for their water molecules,” said Siqi Zou, PhD'24, co-first author of the study and former UChicago PME graduate student.
sentences.zh: 该研究的共同第一作者、前芝加哥大学 PME 研究生、24 届博士 Siqi Zou 表示：“即使行为几乎相同的元素仍会尝试膨胀材料，为水分子腾出空间。”

sentences.en: “By pinning the channel so it can’t expand at all, we forced that small difference in behavior to become a much bigger difference in how strongly each element binds.”
sentences.zh: “通过固定通道使其根本无法扩展，我们迫使行为上的微小差异变成了每个元素结合强度的更大差异。”

blocks.html_en: With the addition of magnesium, the enrichment of neodymium over lanthanum jumped from a 1.6-fold difference to a 5.4-fold difference. After two cycles of purification, researchers could get a neodymium sample that was 97% pure. Similar improvements were seen for other rare earth elements.

sentences.en: With the addition of magnesium, the enrichment of neodymium over lanthanum jumped from a 1.6-fold difference to a 5.4-fold difference.
sentences.zh: 添加镁后，钕相对于镧的富集度从 1.6 倍跃升至 5.4 倍。

sentences.en: After two cycles of purification, researchers could get a neodymium sample that was 97% pure.
sentences.zh: 经过两个循环的纯化后，研究人员可以获得纯度为 97% 的钕样品。

sentences.en: Similar improvements were seen for other rare earth elements.
sentences.zh: 其他稀土元素也出现了类似的改善。

blocks.html_en:
This kind of separation is competitive with other rare earth separation methods, but it’s done in water, without organic solvents. That’s a difference that could actually matter at manufacturing scale.

George Schatz, professor of chemistry at Northwestern University and a co-author of the study

sentences.en: This kind of separation is competitive with other rare earth separation methods, but it’s done in water, without organic solvents.
sentences.zh: 这种分离与其他稀土分离方法具有竞争力，但它是在水中完成的，无需有机溶剂。

sentences.en: That’s a difference that could actually matter at manufacturing scale.George Schatz, professor of chemistry at Northwestern University and a co-author of the study
sentences.zh: 这种差异在生产规模上实际上可能很重要。 西北大学化学教授、该研究的合著者乔治·沙茨 (George Schatz)

blocks.html_en: This kind of separation is competitive with other rare earth separation methods, but it’s done in water, without organic solvents. That’s a difference that could actually matter at manufacturing scale.

sentences.en: This kind of separation is competitive with other rare earth separation methods, but it’s done in water, without organic solvents.
sentences.zh: 这种分离与其他稀土分离方法具有竞争力，但它是在水中完成的，无需有机溶剂。

sentences.en: That’s a difference that could actually matter at manufacturing scale.
sentences.zh: 这种差异在制造规模上实际上可能很重要。

blocks.html_en: George Schatz, professor of chemistry at Northwestern University and a co-author of the study

sentences.en: George Schatz, professor of chemistry at Northwestern University and a co-author of the study
sentences.zh: 乔治·沙茨（George Schatz），西北大学化学教授，该研究的合著者

blocks.html_en: A cleaner future

sentences.en: A cleaner future
sentences.zh: 更清洁的未来

blocks.html_en: The new purification method could ultimately point toward different ways of thinking about where rare earth processing happens, the researchers said.

sentences.en: The new purification method could ultimately point toward different ways of thinking about where rare earth processing happens, the researchers said.
sentences.zh: 研究人员表示，新的提纯方法最终可能会为稀土加工的不同思考方式指明方向。

blocks.html_en: “Right now, rare earth ores get mined all over the world, but almost all of them end up being sent overseas for processing,” said Schatz, co-author of the study. “A method like this, that just uses water and electricity instead of organic solvents, is the kind of technology that could actually change how and where that processing gets done.”

sentences.en: “Right now, rare earth ores get mined all over the world, but almost all of them end up being sent overseas for processing,” said Schatz, co-author of the study.
sentences.zh: 该研究的合著者沙茨表示：“目前，稀土矿石在世界各地开采，但几乎所有矿石最终都被送往海外进行加工。”

sentences.en: “A method like this, that just uses water and electricity instead of organic solvents, is the kind of technology that could actually change how and where that processing gets done.”
sentences.zh: “像这样的方法，只使用水和电而不是有机溶剂，这种技术实际上可以改变处理的方式和地点。”

blocks.html_en: The method, however, isn’t yet ready to be scaled up to replace industrial purification. Still, it points to a broader design principle: tuning a channel’s width can determine which ions a material prefers, even for ions that differ by a fraction of an angstrom.

sentences.en: The method, however, isn’t yet ready to be scaled up to replace industrial purification.
sentences.zh: 然而，该方法尚未准备好扩大规模以取代工业纯化。

sentences.en: Still, it points to a broader design principle: tuning a channel’s width can determine which ions a material prefers, even for ions that differ by a fraction of an angstrom.
sentences.zh: 尽管如此，它还是指向了一个更广泛的设计原则：调整通道的宽度可以确定材料更喜欢哪些离子，即使是相差零点几埃的离子。

blocks.html_en: Chong Liu’s group is now testing the approach against more of the 15 lanthanides, while Schatz is refining his computational models, aiming to explain the pinning effect more quantitatively. He is also using the same modeling approach developed for this work to study other materials.

sentences.en: Chong Liu’s group is now testing the approach against more of the 15 lanthanides, while Schatz is refining his computational models, aiming to explain the pinning effect more quantitatively.
sentences.zh: Chong Liu 的团队目前正在针对 15 种镧系元素中的更多元素测试该方法，而 Schatz 正在完善他的计算模型，旨在更定量地解释钉扎效应。

sentences.en: He is also using the same modeling approach developed for this work to study other materials.
sentences.zh: 他还使用为这项工作开发的相同建模方法来研究其他材料。

blocks.html_en: Citation: “Pinning Angstrom-size solid ionic channel for the separation of rare earth elements,” Zou et al, Nature Chemical Engineering, July 21, 2026, DOI: 10.1038/s44286-026-00418-8

sentences.en: Citation: “Pinning Angstrom-size solid ionic channel for the separation of rare earth elements,” Zou et al, Nature Chemical Engineering, July 21, 2026, DOI: 10.1038/s44286-026-00418-8
sentences.zh: 引文：“用于分离稀土元素的钉扎埃尺寸固体离子通道”，Zou 等人，《自然化学工程》，2026 年 7 月 21 日，DOI：10.1038/s44286-026-00418-8

content_html_zh:
稀土元素（如镧、钕和镝）用于制造汽车中的电动机、房屋中的 LED 灯以及医生办公室中的 MRI 机器。 但首先，必须对它们进行开采并相互分离。 从历史上看，这种净化是一个困难且成本高昂的过程，依赖大量有毒化学品。

现在，副实验室的研究人员。 芝加哥大学普利兹克分子工程学院 (UChicago PME) 的 Chong Liu 教授与西北大学和阿贡国家实验室的同事合作，发现了一种更清洁的方法来分离稀土元素。

新方法依赖于层状形式的氧化锰——一种具有适当尺寸层的矿物材料，允许离子滑入和滑出并区分稀土元素。

这项发表在《自然化学工程》上的新研究的资深作者刘说：“这是人们第一次使用电化学插层并利用结构特征来分离类似的镧系元素，这些元素本质上很难分离。” “同样有价值的是，我们对稀土离子如何与这种材料相互作用以及我们如何操纵它以获得更好的选择性提供了许多新的理解。”

“这种分离方法与其他稀土分离方法具有竞争力，但它是在水中完成的，没有有机溶剂，”西北大学化学教授、该研究的合著者乔治·沙茨(George Schatz)说。 “这种差异在制造规模上实际上可能很重要。”

这是人们第一次利用电化学插层并利用其结构特征来分离本质上很难分离的相似的镧系元素。 该研究的资深作者Chong Liu教授

通过通道挤压元件

17 种稀土元素（包括 15 种镧系元素，加上钪和钇）很少单独出现。 它们几乎总是一起开采，从化学角度来看，它们几乎是相同的，只有离子大小和酸度的微小差异来区分每一种。 将它们分开通常需要定制的分子和大量的酸，酸用于从这些分子上剥离每种元素。

这篇新论文的共同第一作者、芝加哥大学 PME 研究生刘嘉栋表示：“稀土总是混合在一起，无论是在矿石中还是在废物流中，即使将它们从其他东西中分离出来，将它们彼此分离也是第二个非常具有挑战性的步骤。”

刘冲和她的同事知道，稀土离子之间的区别之一是当它们溶解在溶液中时，每个稀土离子周围的水壳的大小。 镧等较轻的稀土元素具有较大的第一水壳层，而镝等较重的稀土元素具有较小的第一水壳层。

利用这种尺寸差异，Chong Liu 的团队设计了氧化锰，使其堆叠层之间的间隙只有几个水分子宽。 然后，他们将稀土元素的原始混合物挤压到里面。

该方法将元素分为两组。 较重的镧系元素和较小的水壳更紧密地卡在通道中。 具有较大壳层的较轻的镧系元素将这些层推开，从而松开了它们的抓地力。

为了确认分子水平上发生的情况，芝加哥大学 PME 团队与西北大学的 Schatz 团队合作，使用一种称为密度泛函理论的方法进行量子力学模拟，该方法根据基础物理原理预测原子如何排列自身并相互作用。 他们还与阿贡科学家合作获得实验 X 射线数据。

“看到我们的密度泛函理论计算与同步加速器 X 射线测量结果如此接近，这是令人难以置信的回报，”共同第一作者、西北大学 George Schatz 实验室的博士后研究员 Woo Cheol Jeon 说。 “计算让我们逐个原子地了解每种稀土元素如何在有限通道内排列其水合壳，这是实验无法直接解决的。”

微调净化

虽然新工艺分离了最重和最轻的稀土，但一些最有用的元素仍然表现得太相似而无法分离。 为了微调纯化过程，使其能够区分相似的稀土元素对，研究小组使用了电流并添加了镁离子。

镁充当支架，将氧化锰通道保持在设计的间距内，即使稀土元素试图扩大它，这种效应称为钉扎。 现在，稀土离子在与层状材料的结合方面表现出差异，即使它们极其相似。

该研究的共同第一作者、前芝加哥大学 PME 研究生、24 届博士 Siqi Zou 表示：“即使行为几乎相同的元素仍会尝试膨胀材料，为水分子腾出空间。” “通过固定通道使其根本无法扩展，我们迫使行为上的微小差异变成了每个元素结合强度的更大差异。”

添加镁后，钕相对于镧的富集度从 1.6 倍跃升至 5.4 倍。 经过两个循环的纯化后，研究人员可以获得纯度为 97% 的钕样品。 其他稀土元素也出现了类似的改善。

这种分离与其他稀土分离方法具有竞争力，但它是在水中完成的，无需有机溶剂。 这种差异在生产规模上实际上可能很重要。 西北大学化学教授、该研究的合著者乔治·沙茨 (George Schatz)

更清洁的未来

研究人员表示，新的提纯方法最终可能会为稀土加工的不同思考方式指明方向。

该研究的合著者沙茨表示：“目前，稀土矿石在世界各地开采，但几乎所有矿石最终都被送往海外进行加工。” “像这样的方法，只使用水和电而不是有机溶剂，这种技术实际上可以改变处理的方式和地点。”

然而，该方法尚未准备好扩大规模以取代工业纯化。 尽管如此，它还是指向了一个更广泛的设计原则：调整通道的宽度可以确定材料更喜欢哪些离子，即使是相差零点几埃的离子。

Chong Liu 的团队目前正在针对 15 种镧系元素中的更多元素测试该方法，而 Schatz 正在完善他的计算模型，旨在更定量地解释钉扎效应。 他还使用为这项工作开发的相同建模方法来研究其他材料。

引文：“用于分离稀土元素的钉扎埃尺寸固体离子通道”，Zou 等人，《自然化学工程》，2026 年 7 月 21 日，DOI：10.1038/s44286-026-00418-8
